title: Hylotelephium pluricaule
intro: Hylotelephium pluricaule, also known as Korean stonecrop, is a perennial plant species in the family Crassulaceae, characterized by its thick, fleshy leaves and vibrant purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Korean medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Hylotelephium pluricaule include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hylotelephium pluricaule is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture to take advantage of its purported health benefits.
taxonomy: Hylotelephium pluricaule is classified under the family Crassulaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the stonecrop family. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic relationships with other species within the family. Hylotelephium pluricaule is further grouped into the genus Hylotelephium, which consists of several species of succulent plants native to East Asia. The genus Hylotelephium is part of the broader order Saxifragales, which includes a diverse range of flowering plants. At the kingdom level, Hylotelephium pluricaule is classified as Plantae, indicating its status as a plant species.
